title: Cottage Media Console
summary: A two-shelf media console with a center drawer flanked by open pass-through bays — leave the back panel off and both open bays run straight through, front to back.
description:
This console shares its 1x2/1x3 laminated-leg technique with this catalog's simple nightstand, scaled wider into a low media stand with two open side bays and a center drawer. Mark and attach the side legs to the shelves first, then add the front and back legs — nails and glue are fine here, or pocket holes if you'd rather hide the fasteners entirely under paint.

Trim both shelves flush on top with 1x2 shelf trim; the bottom shelf's trim is optional but the top shelf needs it to look finished. **The center drawer box is built directly into the case** between two 1x12 dividers, with drawer guides fastened flush to the inside of the legs — not the inside of the drawer box itself — so double-check which surface the guide rides against before nailing it down.

**Leaving the plywood back panel off turns the open side bays into genuine pass-throughs** — useful if you want cables, a router, or a game console to sit behind the piece with the console itself acting as a low media shelf rather than a closed cabinet. Build the drawer square to its opening, center the drawer face on the opening with the box held in place from inside the cabinet, then nail the face on from the front.

materials.name: Self-tapping wood screws, 2", or pocket hole screws, 1-1/4"
Paint, primer or stain and poly

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut two shelves at 44-1/2", two drawer box dividers at 5-3/4", one top at 48" and two drawer bottoms at 18" from the 1x12. Cut four side legs at 17-1/4", four shelf trim at 41", four side shelf trim at 8-1/2", four drawer guides at 11-1/2", two drawer box center trim at 5-3/4" and two top trim at 48" from the 1x2. Cut four front/back legs at 17-1/4" from the 1x3. Cut one back panel (optional) at 46" from the 1/4" plywood, panel for back (optional). Cut two drawer backs at 18", four drawer sides at 12-1/4" and two drawer fronts at 19-1/2" from the 1x6. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to two 8 ft 1x12, five 8 ft 1x2, one 8 ft 1x3 and one 12 ft 1x6. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw.

steps.title: Attach the side legs
steps.body: Mark the side legs and attach them to the shelves with glue and 2" screws or pocket hole screws.
materials: Self-tapping wood screws, 2", or pocket hole screws, 1-1/4"

steps.title: Attach the front and back legs
steps.body: Attach the front and back legs with finish nails and glue, or screws if you don't have a nailer.

steps.title: Trim the shelves
steps.body: Attach the shelf trim and side shelf trim to both shelves with a nailer, keeping the shelf tops flush. Trim can be left off the bottom shelf if desired.

steps.title: Build the drawer box
steps.body: Attach the drawer box dividers and center trim in place with brad nails and glue, forming the center drawer opening.

steps.title: Add the drawer guides
steps.body: Attach the drawer guides flush to the inside faces of the legs so the drawer runs straight.

steps.title: Attach the top
steps.body: Attach the top, overhanging about 1" on the sides and front, then nail on the top trim with 2" finish nails and glue.

steps.title: Add the back panel (optional)
steps.body: Attach the plywood back panel if you want a closed cabinet look. Leave it off to let the side bays pass straight through.

steps.title: Build the drawers
steps.body: Attach the drawer backs to the drawer bottoms, then attach the sides to the back and bottom of each drawer box.

steps.title: Fit the drawer faces
steps.body: Place each drawer box inside its opening without the face attached, center the face over the opening for an even gap, then nail it on with 2" finish nails and glue.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Remove the drawers and fill all holes with wood filler, applying two coats since filler shrinks as it dries. Sand with 120 grit sandpaper, then prime and paint or stain as desired. Add knobs.
materials: Paint, primer or stain and poly

images.alt: Cottage media console with a center drawer and open side bays
